item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a history and mythology nerd, I enjoyed this very much. I also like how you chose a mythology that doesn't see much coverage in any kind of media. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I maintain that both "In Media Resin" and "A Sappy Love Story" are infinitely better titles, with "Resin D'etre" as a close second. Regardless of that small lapse in judgement on your parts, great job with this :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I managed to guess where it was going pretty early on, but the route it took to get there ended up being a wild ride regardless. Kudos to the authors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops, sorry, thank you for noticing.

Thank you for the +1 anyway!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s incredibly nice of you to say! Thank you sk much, I really appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is beautiful. I felt myself getting pulled into Dr Schrader's excitement as he was regaling the epic of the Bear-Slayer.

I had some problems following the pronouns in the logs - I think you refer to Dr Schrader as "her" a couple of times - but that's just a quibble. Instant +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent job. You did an amazing job of creating an impression of a rich and interesting civilization with in-depth mythology, tied Latvian mythology into SCP lore, wrote interesting and believable characters that felt human, and overall created an amazingly well-written and enjoyable SCP. If I could +1 more than once, I would. I think this is going to become one of my favorite SCPs.

item.title: Re: Reserved for OTC Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Well, You guys' hard work seems to have paid off! This skip is pretty awesome and told a really cool story. My main gripe with it is that the story about Lāčplēsis and the bear knights seemed very expositiony but I don't really see a way to change that without damaging the flow of the story.

+1

item.title: Rimple's Author Post
item.content:encoded:
what Othello said

ok actually
– hide block

It's been sick as hell working with Othello - they've got rad ideas and they're fun to brainstorm loudly with.

Thanks to Randomini and TheGreatSea for crit. Thanks to my Dad as always for putting up with me endlessly infodumping about this as I do with all my writing.

First two images are from wikidot commons. Map was generated with this free-to-use generator and edited by PeppersGhost.

item.title: Reserved for OTC Author Post
item.content:encoded:
After months upon months of working with Rimple to complete this skip, we've finally finished. I've always been passionate about having a Latvian skip in the database, considering the fact that there are literally zero of them, and I particularly wanted to involve the Latvian Demi-god Lāčplēsis, arguably one of the cooler mythological figures in Latvian folklore.

Between the society revolving around amber, jokes about bread and beer and singing, and even a few Latvian puns, this is a skip I am incredibly passionate about, and I couldn't have done it without the help of Rimple. He has been patient with our difficult time zone differences, and has always been willing to help me learn and grow my ideas until they could reach the point they have.

So from me, a Latvian person, please enjoy the first ever Latvian skip.
